basics.name: Yue Zhao
basics.label: Undergraduate Student
basics.summary: I am an undergraduate student majoring in Artificial Intelligence at Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications, with a strong interest in Reinforcement Learning, Natural Language Processing, and their intersection. I have excellent academic performance (average score of 92, top 3.9% in major, top 2% in college) and have excelled in over 50 courses with scores above 90, including several core courses with scores above 95. I possess strong self-learning capabilities, research and engineering practice abilities, teamwork spirit, communication skills, and a positive attitude. I have rich experience in scientific research, having participated in multiple projects and contributed innovative optimization methods.

work.name: THUNLP
work.summary: Explored and implemented a method for obtaining structured representations of Internet data, significantly improving the collection and structuring of multimodal data. Developed techniques that reduced error rates by over 50% in large-scale multimodal datasets. Participated in the VisCPM project, contributing to the construction of multilingual multimodal datasets using the M2M100 model.

work.name: Intelligent Science and Technology Center, Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications
work.summary: Proposed a novel image harmonization method based on diffusion models. Implemented and conducted experiments for generating harmonized images using CLIP and diffusion models. Actively involved in experimental design and paper writing.

education.institution: Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications
education.score: 92, 3.8/4.0, top 3.9% in major, top 2% in college

publications.name: Large Multilingual Models Pivot Zero-shot Multimodal Learning Across Languages. In ICLR 2024 (Spotlight).
publications.summary: Proposed an effective training paradigm, MPM, for non-English large multimodal models, demonstrating their ability to generalize across languages in zero-shot multimodal learning tasks, even surpassing native language models trained on multimodal data.

publications.name: Reformulating Vision-Language Foundation Models and Datasets Towards Universal Multimodal Assistants. Preprint.
publications.summary: Introduced Muffin framework and UniMM-Chat dataset, leveraging pre-trained vision-language models as visual signal providers for generating high-quality and diverse multimodal instructions, transforming and enhancing the knowledge intensiveness of conversation data.

skills.name: Table Tennis, Tennis, Badminton, Swimming, Cricket

projects.name: ReinforceLearning GitHub Project
projects.summary: A collection of implemented reinforcement learning algorithms, including DQN, AC, A2C, TRPO, PPO, etc., trained and verified in the CartPole environment.
highlights: Verified algorithms' performance in CartPole environment

projects.name: IMAGECAPTION GitHub Project
projects.summary: Implementation of common image captioning models using PyTorch, including CNN+RNN, ViT+Transformer. Employed LLaVA for re-captioning to generate diverse image descriptions.

projects.name: Diffusion Model for Cartoon Character
projects.summary: Developed a diffusion model to generate cartoon character faces, using a custom-built anime character dataset. Preprocessed the dataset with face recognition algorithms for optimal model training.
highlights: Built and preprocessed anime character dataset
Implemented diffusion model for character face generation

projects.name: Matrix Multiplication Optimization
projects.summary: Optimized matrix multiplication algorithms, including block, parallel, and vectorized methods, with implementations in C++ and optimizations using OpenMP and SIMD instructions.
highlights: Implemented and tested various optimization techniques

projects.name: LLM-CliHelper GitHub Project
projects.summary: A command-line agent based on large language models, supporting tasks such as code writing, project implementation, knowledge querying, and code execution.

projects.name: OFA for Chemical
projects.summary: Modeled chemical reaction formula generation as a Visual QA problem, training the OFA model on chemical reaction datasets to generate structurally sound results.
